name: Greek Croustade
recipeIngredient: 10 Phyllo dough sheets, thawed (17x12 inches each)
1/2 cup Butter or margarine, melted (1 stick)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook spinach according to directions; drain well, pressing out excess liquid.
2. In a saucepan, cook onion and garlic in 3 tablespoons butter or margarine until tender.
3. Stir in flour, tarragon, and pepper. Add milk all at once. Cook and stir until the mixture is thickened.
4. Stir half of the mixture into eggs; return egg mixture to the remaining sauce. Add cheeses and spinach; set aside.
5. Unroll phyllo dough; cover with a dampened towel. Remove 1 sheet of dough and brush lightly with some of the melted butter.
6. Fold the sheet in thirds lengthwise; brush the top with butter.
7. Place one end of the folded sheet in the center of a 12- or 14-inch pizza pan, extending it over the side of the pan. Repeat buttering of remaining phyllo dough, arranging strips spoke fashion evenly around the pan. The ends of sheets will overlap in the center and be approximately 3 inches apart at the outer ends.
8. Spread spinach mixture in an 8-inch circle in the center of the pastry.
9. Starting with the last sheet of dough placed in the pan, lift the end of a leaf up and bring toward the center of the filling. Holding the end with both hands, twist several times; coil and tuck the end under to form a rosette.
10. Lay the rosette over the filling, leaving a 3-inch circle in the center (the center should be visible). Repeat with remaining sheets in reverse order they were placed.
11. Drizzle remaining butter over all.
12. Bake in a 375 degree oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until golden. Serve warm or cool. Makes 10 to 12 servings. Preparation time: 20 minutes, Baking time: 35 to 40 minutes
